name: Mick Goodrick
abstract: Mick Goodrick (June 9, 1945 – November 16, 2022) was an American jazz guitarist who spent most of his career as a teacher. In the early 1970s, he worked with Gary Burton and Pat Metheny.

concerts.label: Kool Jazz Festival: Charlie Haden's Liberation Music Orchestra/Philly Joe Jones' Dameronia

about_quotes.speaker: John Abercrombie
about_quotes.evidence: one time we had a band with myself and Mick Goodrick, also a great guitar player, and Ron McClure.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Abercrombie interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 19, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: I had been working with a group with John and Mick Goodrick... Mick Goodrick was still in it.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jim Hall
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...you had Mick Goodrick, John Abercrombie… [INTERVIEWEE] Oh, yeah.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jim Hall — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: it had Mick Goodrick on guitar... I did on oh it was another label
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 7, 2025
